text.title: Gce Autoscaling Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the gce-autoscaling methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Queue depth grows while CPU stays flat; tail latency blows up.
antipattern.root-cause: MIG autoscaling on CPU metric for a worker fed by Pub/Sub.
antipattern.fix: Switch to custom-metric autoscaling on subscription `num_undelivered_messages`.

antipattern.symptom: Morning ramp-up causes elevated latency for first 15 min.
antipattern.fix: Add scaling schedule profile for known traffic peaks.

antipattern.symptom: Cold start visible to users every quiet period.
antipattern.fix: Set `--min-num-replicas=1` (or higher); document if 0 is intentional.
